subject: fix(theme): strengthen Uniwind migration — cleanup, correctness, docs
body:
Quality pass over the NativeWind → Uniwind migration:

Fixes:
- useTheme.ts: Fix stale closure in toggleMode via functional setState
- useTheme.ts: Add error handling on AsyncStorage operations
- useTheme.ts: Extract applyTheme helper to avoid duplicated Uniwind calls
- themeColors.ts: withAlpha returns rgba() instead of 8-digit hex for
  broader React Native style context compatibility
- global.css: Add backward-compat aliases to named fantasy/luxury variants
  (not just default light/dark) so old class names work regardless of
  which theme path Uniwind resolves

Cleanup:
- Remove stale nativewind-env.d.ts and nativewind-end.d.ts type files
- Update tsconfig.json to reference uniwind-types.d.ts
- Update hardcoded old theme colors in app/+html.tsx (#0A0A0A → #09090b,
  #C9A57B → #ffffff, etc.) to match luxury DaisyUI theme
- Update public/manifest.json theme_color and background_color
- Fix package.json description (NativeWind → Uniwind)

Documentation:
- CLAUDE.md: Rewrite Tech Stack, Theme & Colors, Styling System sections
  with DaisyUI architecture, Uniwind limitation notes, migration mapping
- CLAUDE.md: Add useTheme.ts and themeColors.ts to Project Structure
- SESSION_NOTES.md: Full session summary with decisions and next steps
- HISTORY.md: Add migration entry
- TODO.md: Add Phase 3 migration tasks and theme toggle

Tags: theme, daisyui, uniwind, cleanup, docs, code-quality
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-system
Semver: patch
